title: Result - Austria
content:
Welcome to Austria, a land of stunning diversity, from snowy peaks and glaciers to lakes, vineyards and rolling green hills. Austrians, like Canadians, cherish the outdoors, enjoy hiking, climbing, water sports and world-class skiing.
Rich in tradition, Austrians often wear Dirndls or Lederhosen for special occasions. Music enthusiasts will be inspired by Austria’s legendary composers like Mozart, Haydn and Schubert.

## Unique food tradition
Apfelstrudel (apple strudel): a baked rolled pastry usually filled with apples.
Most people in Austria work in the service sector (74%), followed by industry (23%) and agriculture (3%).

### Local legend or myth
Krampus: a devil-like figure from folklore who punishes naughty kids by giving coal at Christmas while Saint Nicholas rewards the good ones.

### Language of the Land
Austria recognizes Croatian, Slovenian, Hungarian, Czech, Slovak and Romani as minority languages, protected under the Ethnic Groups Act to preserve their languages and customs.
UNESCO World Heritage sites are located in Austria, including Vienna's historic centre, which draws millions of tourists annually.
Ready to make your plans to work and travel abroad? Take the first step by learning more about your selected country and choose the work permit that's right for you.
